   466→            # Only set project_qty for root assemblies (level 1)
   467→            # Child items (level 2+) get project_qty=0, their requirement comes from bom_qty_required
   468→            level = node.get("level", 0)
   469→            project_qty = node.get("qty", 0) if level == 1 else 0
   470→
   471→            cm_data = {
   472→                "doctype": "Project Component Master",
   473→                "project": project,
   474→                "item_code": item_code,
   475→                "machine_code": machine_code,  # Set machine code on all Component Masters
   476→                "bom_level": level,
   477→                "is_loose_item": 0,
   478→                "design_status": "Design Released",
   479→                "project_qty": project_qty,  # From Excel column E (root assembly only)
   480→                "created_from": "BOM Upload",
   481→                "released_for_procurement": released_for_procurement,
   482→            }
   483→
   484→            # Determine Make/Buy based on item code prefix
   485→            # Items starting with M or G: "Make" (assemblies/sub-assemblies)
   486→            # All other items: "Buy" (raw materials/purchased components)
   487→            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
   488→            if item_code_upper.startswith(("M", "G")):
   489→                default_make_or_buy = "Make"
   490→            else:
   491→                default_make_or_buy = "Buy"
   492→
   493→            if is_assembly:
   494→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 1
   495→                cm_data["active_bom"] = None  # Set later after BOM creation
   496→                cm_data["bom_structure_hash"] = calculate_tree_structure_hash(node["children"])
   497→                # Excel value takes precedence, otherwise use prefix-based default
   498→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or default_make_or_buy
   499→            else:
   500→                # Leaf item (raw material) - use prefix-based default
   501→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 0
   502→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or default_make_or_buy
   503→
   504→            cm = frappe.get_doc(cm_data)
   505→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
   506→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
   507→            cm.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
   508→            counters["created"] += 1
   509→        except Exception:
   510→            counters["failed"] += 1
   511→            frappe.log_error(
   512→                title=f"Failed to create Component Master for {item_code}",
   513→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
   514→            )
   515→
   516→    return counters
   517→
   518→
   519→def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):
   520→    """
   521→    Merge make/buy from Excel into existing Component Master.
   522→    Only updates if Excel has an explicit value; otherwise keeps existing.
   523→
   524→    This allows PE2 team to only tag new items or changes in the Excel.
   525→    Previously tagged items can be left blank.
   526→
   527→    Returns:
   528→        bool: True if value was updated, False if unchanged
   529→    """
   530→    excel_make_or_buy = node.get("make_or_buy")
   531→    if not excel_make_or_buy:
   532→        return False  # No value in Excel, keep existing
   533→
   534→    current_value = frappe.db.get_value(
   535→        "Project Component Master", cm_name, "make_or_buy"
   536→    )
   537→
   538→    if current_value != excel_make_or_buy:
   539→        frappe.db.set_value(
   540→            "Project Component Master", cm_name,
   541→            "make_or_buy", excel_make_or_buy
   542→        )
   543→        return True
   544→    return False
   545→
   546→
   547→def _get_all_nodes(tree):
   548→    """Flatten tree to get ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)."""
   549→    all_nodes = []
   550→    for node in tree:
   551→        all_nodes.append(node)
   552→        if node.get("children"):
   553→            all_nodes.extend(_get_all_nodes(node["children"]))
   554→    return all_nodes
   555→
   556→
   557→def _get_assembly_nodes(tree):
   558→    """Flatten tree to get all assembly nodes (nodes that have children)."""
   559→    assemblies = []
   560→    for node in tree:
   561→        if node.get("children"):
   562→            assemblies.append(node)
   563→            assemblies.extend(_get_assembly_nodes(node["children"]))
   564→    return assemblies
   565→
   566→
   567→# ==================== Step 4: Analyze Upload ====================
   568→
   569→def analyze_upload(tree, project, machine_code):
   570→    """
   571→    Analyze upload tree for new, unchanged, changed, and blocked components.
   572→
   573→    Args:
   574→        tree: List of root nodes
   575→        project: Project name
   576→        machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload
   577→
   578→    Returns:
   579→        dict with can_create, changed_components, loose_blocked,
   580→        blocked_by_dependencies, summary
   581→    """
   582→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
   583→    graph = _build_dependency_graph(all_assemblies)
   584→
   585→    categorized = {
   586→        "new": [],
   587→        "unchanged": [],
   588→        "changed": [],
   589→        "loose_blocked": [],
   590→    }
   591→
   592→    for item_code, data in graph.items():
   593→        status, details = _determine_component_status(
   594→            item_code, data["node"], project, machine_code
   595→        )
   596→        data["status"] = status
   597→        data["details"] = details
   598→        categorized[status].append(item_code)
   599→
   600→    # Find blocking dependencies (ancestor chain)
   601→    changed_set = set(categorized["changed"])
   602→    loose_blocked_set = set(categorized["loose_blocked"])
   603→    blocked_by_changes = _find_blocked_ancestors(
   604→        graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set
   605→    )
   606→
   607→    # Build list of items safe to create
   608→    blocked_set = set(blocked_by_changes.keys())
   609→    can_create = []
   610→    for item_code in categorized["new"] + categorized["unchanged"]:
   611→        if item_code not in blocked_set:
   612→            can_create.append(graph[item_code]["node"])
   613→
   614→    return {
   615→        "can_create": can_create,
   616→        "changed_components": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["changed"]],
   617→        "loose_blocked": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["loose_blocked"]],
   618→        "blocked_by_dependencies": blocked_by_changes,
   619→        "summary": {
   620→            "total": len(all_assemblies),
   621→            "new": len(categorized["new"]),
   622→            "unchanged": len(categorized["unchanged"]),
   623→            "changed": len(categorized["changed"]),
   624→            "loose_blocked": len(categorized["loose_blocked"]),
   625→            "can_create": len(can_create),
   626→            "blocked": len(blocked_by_changes),
   627→        },
   628→    }
   629→
   630→
   631→def _determine_component_status(item_code, node, project, machine_code):
   632→    """
   633→    Determine if component is new, unchanged, changed, or loose_blocked.
   634→
   635→    Args:
   636→        item_code: Item code to check
   637→        node: Tree node dict
   638→        project: Project name
   639→        machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload
   640→
   641→    Returns:
   642→        tuple: (status, details)
   643→    """
   644→    component_master = frappe.db.get_value(
   645→        "Project Component Master",
   646→        {
   647→            "project": project,
   648→            "item_code": item_code,
   649→            "machine_code": machine_code
   650→        },
   651→        ["name", "is_loose_item", "can_be_converted_to_bom",
   652→         "bom_structure_hash", "active_bom"],
   653→        as_dict=True,
   654→    )
   655→
   656→    if not component_master:
   657→        return "new", {"first_time": True}
   658→
   659→    # Check loose item blocking
   660→    if component_master.is_loose_item and not component_master.can_be_converted_to_bom:
   661→        return "loose_blocked", {
   662→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   663→            "message": f"Loose item {item_code} not enabled for BOM conversion",
   664→        }
   665→
   666→    # Check for BOM changes — no active BOM linked to Component Master yet
   667→    if not component_master.active_bom:
   668→        # But check if an active BOM already exists in system (might not be linked yet)
   669→        existing_bom = frappe.db.get_value(
   670→            "BOM",
   671→            {
   672→                "item": item_code,
   673→                "project": project,
   674→                "is_active": 1,
   675→                "is_default": 1,
   676→                "docstatus": 1,
   677→            },
   678→            "name",
   679→        )
   680→
   681→        if not existing_bom:
   682→            # Also check without project filter (BOM may not have project set)
   683→            existing_bom = frappe.db.get_value(
   684→                "BOM",
   685→                {
   686→                    "item": item_code,
   687→                    "is_active": 1,
   688→                    "is_default": 1,
   689→                    "docstatus": 1,
   690→                },
   691→                "name",
   692→            )
   693→
   694→        if not existing_bom:
   695→            # No existing BOM found — truly new
   696→            return "new", {
   697→                "component_master": component_master.name,
   698→                "first_time": False,
   699→            }
   700→
   701→        # BOM exists but not linked to Component Master — compare hashes
   702→        children = node.get("children", [])
   703→        new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   704→
   705→        existing_hash = frappe.db.get_value(
   706→            "BOM", existing_bom, "custom_bom_structure_hash"
   707→        )
   708→
   709→        # Backfill hash if NULL
   710→        if not existing_hash:
   711→            bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", existing_bom)
   712→            existing_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
   713→            if existing_hash:
   714→                frappe.db.set_value(
   715→                    "BOM", existing_bom,
   716→                    "custom_bom_structure_hash", existing_hash,
   717→                    update_modified=False
   718→                )
   719→
   720→        if new_hash == existing_hash:
   721→            # BOM exists and hash matches — unchanged, will be linked later
   722→            return "unchanged", {
   723→                "component_master": component_master.name,
   724→                "bom": existing_bom,
   725→                "existing_bom_not_linked": True,
   726→            }
   727→        else:
   728→            # BOM exists but hash differs — version change needed
   729→            blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, existing_bom)
   730→            children = node.get("children", [])
   731→            bom_diff = calculate_bom_diff(existing_bom, children)
   732→            return "changed", {
   733→                "change_type": "bom_structure",
   734→                "component_master": component_master.name,
   735→                "old_bom": existing_bom,
   736→                "old_hash": existing_hash,
   737→                "new_hash": new_hash,
   738→                "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
   739→                "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
   740→                "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
   741→                "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
   742→                "existing_bom_not_linked": True,
   743→                "bom_diff": bom_diff,
   744→            }
   745→
   746→    # Compare BOM structures via hash - get hash from BOM itself (source of truth)
   747→    children = node.get("children", [])
   748→    new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   749→
   750→    # Get existing hash from BOM (not Component Master)
   751→    existing_hash = frappe.db.get_value(
   752→        "BOM", component_master.active_bom, "custom_bom_structure_hash"
   753→    )
   754→
   755→    # Backfill if NULL - calculate from actual BOM items
   756→    if not existing_hash:
   757→        bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", component_master.active_bom)
   758→        existing_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
   759→        if existing_hash:
   760→            # Store the backfilled hash on BOM
   761→            frappe.db.set_value(
   762→                "BOM", component_master.active_bom,
   763→                "custom_bom_structure_hash", existing_hash,
   764→                update_modified=False
   765→            )
   766→
   767→    if new_hash != existing_hash:
   768→        # Check procurement blocking level
   769→        blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(
   770→            project, item_code, component_master.active_bom
   771→        )
   772→        bom_diff = calculate_bom_diff(component_master.active_bom, children)
   773→        return "changed", {
   774→            "change_type": "bom_structure",
   775→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   776→            "old_bom": component_master.active_bom,
   777→            "old_hash": existing_hash,
   778→            "new_hash": new_hash,
   779→            "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
   780→            "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
   781→            "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
   782→            "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
   783→            "bom_diff": bom_diff,
   784→        }
   785→
   786→    return "unchanged", {
   787→        "component_master": component_master.name,
   788→        "bom": component_master.active_bom,
   789→    }
   790→
   791→
   792→def _build_dependency_graph(assemblies):
   793→    """
   794→    Build a dependency graph from assembly nodes.
   795→    Maps item_code -> {node, children (item_codes), status, details}
   796→    """
   797→    graph = {}
   798→    for node in assemblies:
   799→        child_codes = [c["item_code"] for c in node.get("children", [])]
   800→        graph[node["item_code"]] = {
   801→            "node": node,
   802→            "children": child_codes,
   803→            "status": None,
   804→            "details": None,
   805→        }
   806→    return graph
   807→
   808→
   809→def _find_blocked_ancestors(graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set):
   810→    """
   811→    Find all components blocked by changed or loose-blocked children.
   812→    Uses iterative approach to propagate blocking up the tree.
   813→
   814→    Returns:
   815→        dict: Mapping of blocked item codes to list of blocking reasons
   816→    """
   817→    blocked = {}
   818→    # Multiple passes to propagate transitive blocking
   819→    changed = True
   820→    while changed:
   821→        changed = False
   822→        for item_code, data in graph.items():
   823→            if item_code in blocked or item_code in changed_set or item_code in loose_blocked_set:
   824→                continue
   825→
   826→            blocking_children = []
   827→            for child_code in data["children"]:
   828→                if child_code in changed_set:
   829→                    blocking_children.append({
   830→                        "item": child_code,
   831→                        "reason": "BOM structure changed",
   832→                    })
   833→                elif child_code in loose_blocked_set:
   834→                    blocking_children.append({
   835→                        "item": child_code,
   836→                        "reason": "Loose item conversion not enabled",
   837→                    })
   838→                elif child_code in blocked:
   839→                    blocking_children.append({
   840→                        "item": child_code,
   841→                        "reason": "Depends on blocked component",
   842→                    })
   843→
   844→            if blocking_children:
   845→                blocked[item_code] = blocking_children
   846→                changed = True
   847→
   848→    return blocked
   849→
   850→
   851→def _get_procurement_documents(project, item_code):
   852→    """
   853→    Get active procurement documents for a component.
   854→    Returns list of dicts with doctype, name, status.
   855→    """
   856→    cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
   857→        "Project Component Master",
   858→        {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   859→        "name",
   860→    )
   861→    if not cm_name:
   862→        return []
   863→
   864→    records = frappe.get_all(
   865→        "Component Procurement Record",
   866→        filters={"parent": cm_name},
   867→        fields=["document_type", "document_name", "status", "quantity"],
   868→    )
   869→
   870→    return [
   871→        {
   872→            "doctype": r.document_type,
   873→            "name": r.document_name,
   874→            "status": r.status or "Unknown",
   875→            "quantity": r.quantity,
   876→        }
   877→        for r in records
   878→    ]
   879→
   880→
   881→# ==================== Phase 4E: Procurement Blocking ====================
   882→
   883→def _get_material_requests(project, item_code):
   884→    """Get active (non-cancelled) Material Requests for the item in the project."""
   885→    return frappe.db.sql("""
   886→        SELECT
   887→            mr.name, mr.docstatus, mr.status,
   888→            mri.qty
   889→        FROM `tabMaterial Request` mr
   890→        INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request Item` mri ON mri.parent = mr.name
   891→        WHERE mri.item_code = %(item_code)s
   892→        AND mr.custom_project_ = %(project)s
   893→        AND mr.docstatus != 2
   894→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)
   895→
   896→
   897→def _get_rfqs(project, item_code):
   898→    """Get active (non-cancelled) RFQs for the item in the project."""
   899→    return frappe.db.sql("""
   900→        SELECT
   901→            rfq.name, rfq.docstatus, rfq.status,
   902→            rfqi.qty
   903→        FROM `tabRequest for Quotation` rfq
   904→        INNER JOIN `tabRequest for Quotation Item` rfqi ON rfqi.parent = rfq.name
   905→        WHERE rfqi.item_code = %(item_code)s
   906→        AND rfq.custom_project = %(project)s
   907→        AND rfq.docstatus != 2
   908→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)
   909→
   910→
   911→def _get_purchase_orders(project, item_code):
   912→    """Get active (non-cancelled) Purchase Orders for the item in the project."""
   913→    return frappe.db.sql("""
   914→        SELECT
   915→            po.name, po.docstatus, po.status,
   916→            poi.qty
   917→        FROM `tabPurchase Order` po
   918→        INNER JOIN `tabPurchase Order Item` poi ON poi.parent = po.name
   919→        WHERE poi.item_code = %(item_code)s
   920→        AND po.project = %(project)s
   921→        AND po.docstatus != 2
   922→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)
   923→
   924→
   925→def _max_blocking_level(current, new_level):
   926→    """Return the stricter blocking level. Order: none < confirm < block < manager_required."""
   927→    levels = {"none": 0, "confirm": 1, "block": 2, "manager_required": 3}
   928→    if levels.get(new_level, 0) > levels.get(current, 0):
   929→        return new_level
   930→    return current
   931→
   932→
   933→def _can_override_po_block():
   934→    """Check if current user has manager role for PO-stage override."""
   935→    user_roles = frappe.get_roles()
   936→    return "Component Master Manager" in user_roles or "System Manager" in user_roles
   937→
   938→
   939→def _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, old_bom_name):
   940→    """
   941→    Check if procurement exists and determine blocking level.
   942→
   943→    Checks children of the old BOM for active MR/RFQ/PO.
   944→
   945→    Returns:
   946→        dict: {
   947→            "can_proceed": bool,
   948→            "blocking_level": "none" | "confirm" | "block" | "manager_required",
   949→            "procurement_docs": [...],
   950→            "message": str
   951→        }
   952→    """
   953→    if not old_bom_name:
   954→        return {
   955→            "can_proceed": True,
   956→            "blocking_level": "none",
   957→            "procurement_docs": [],
   958→            "message": "No existing BOM",
   959→        }
   960→
   961→    # Get all child items from old BOM
   962→    old_bom_items = frappe.get_all(
   963→        "BOM Item",
   964→        filters={"parent": old_bom_name},
   965→        fields=["item_code"]
   966→    )
   967→
   968→    blocking_level = "none"
   969→    procurement_docs = []
   970→
   971→    # Check each child item for procurement
   972→    for bom_item in old_bom_items:
   973→        child_item = bom_item.item_code
   974→
   975→        # Check Material Requests
   976→        mrs = _get_material_requests(project, child_item)
   977→        if mrs:
   978→            for mr in mrs:
   979→                procurement_docs.append({
   980→                    "doctype": "Material Request",
   981→                    "name": mr.name,
   982→                    "status": mr.status or "Draft" if mr.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   983→                    "quantity": mr.qty,
   984→                    "item_code": child_item,
   985→                })
   986→            # MR exists - confirmable (changed from "block" to allow confirmation)
   987→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "confirm")
   988→
   989→        # Check RFQs
   990→        rfqs = _get_rfqs(project, child_item)
   991→        if rfqs:
   992→            for rfq in rfqs:
   993→                procurement_docs.append({
   994→                    "doctype": "Request for Quotation",
   995→                    "name": rfq.name,
   996→                    "status": rfq.status or "Draft" if rfq.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   997→                    "quantity": rfq.qty,
   998→                    "item_code": child_item,
   999→                })
  1000→            # RFQ exists - confirmable (changed from "block" to allow confirmation)
  1001→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "confirm")
  1002→
  1003→        # Check Purchase Orders
  1004→        pos = _get_purchase_orders(project, child_item)
  1005→        if pos:
  1006→            for po in pos:
  1007→                procurement_docs.append({
  1008→                    "doctype": "Purchase Order",
  1009→                    "name": po.name,
  1010→                    "status": po.status or "Draft" if po.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
  1011→                    "quantity": po.qty,
  1012→                    "item_code": child_item,
  1013→                })
  1014→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "manager_required")
  1015→
  1016→    # If no procurement found, still need confirmation for hash change
  1017→    if blocking_level == "none":
  1018→        blocking_level = "confirm"
  1019→
  1020→    # Determine if user can proceed
  1021→    can_proceed = False
  1022→    message = ""
  1023→
  1024→    if blocking_level == "confirm":
  1025→        can_proceed = True
  1026→        message = "BOM structure changed. Confirmation with remarks required."
  1027→    elif blocking_level == "block":
  1028→        can_proceed = False
  1029→        message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Material Requests or RFQs."
  1030→    elif blocking_level == "manager_required":
  1031→        if _can_override_po_block():
  1032→            can_proceed = True
  1033→            message = "Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager override available."
  1034→        else:
  1035→            can_proceed = False
  1036→            message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager role required."
  1037→
  1038→    return {
  1039→        "can_proceed": can_proceed,
  1040→        "blocking_level": blocking_level,
  1041→        "procurement_docs": procurement_docs,
  1042→        "message": message,
  1043→    }
  1044→
  1045→
  1046→@frappe.whitelist()
  1047→def confirm_version_change(docname, confirmations):
  1048→    """
  1049→    Confirm BOM version changes and proceed with upload.
  1050→
  1051→    Args:
  1052→        docname: BOM Upload document name
  1053→        confirmations: JSON string of list of dicts with item_code and remarks
  1054→
  1055→    Returns:
  1056→        dict: Creation summary
  1057→    """
  1058→    if isinstance(confirmations, str):
  1059→        confirmations = json.loads(confirmations)
  1060→
  1061→    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
  1062→    project = doc.project
  1063→    machine_code = doc.machine_code
  1064→
  1065→    if not machine_code:
  1066→        frappe.throw(_("Machine Code is required for BOM Upload"))
  1067→
  1068→    # Store remarks in Component Masters
  1069→    for conf in confirmations:
  1070→        item_code = conf.get("item_code")
  1071→        remarks = conf.get("remarks", "")
  1072→
  1073→        cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1074→            "Project Component Master",
  1075→            {
  1076→                "project": project,
  1077→                "item_code": item_code,
  1078→                "machine_code": machine_code
  1079→            },
  1080→            "name"
  1081→        )
  1082→
  1083→        if cm_name:
  1084→            frappe.db.set_value(
  1085→                "Project Component Master",
  1086→                cm_name,
  1087→                "version_change_remarks",
  1088→                remarks
  1089→            )
  1090→
  1091→    # Re-run the upload, now allowing changed components
  1092→    return _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, [c["item_code"] for c in confirmations])
  1093→
  1094→
  1095→def _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, confirmed_items):
  1096→    """
  1097→    Proceed with BOM creation for confirmed changed items.
  1098→    Deactivates old BOMs and creates new ones.
  1099→    """
  1100→    # Get machine_code from doc
  1101→    machine_code = doc.machine_code
  1102→    if not machine_code:
  1103→        frappe.throw(_("Machine Code is required for BOM Upload"))
  1104→
  1105→    # Parse Excel again
  1106→    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
  1107→    content = file_doc.get_content()
  1108→
  1109→    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
  1110→    ws = wb.active
  1111→
  1112→    # Initialize image loader
  1113→    image_loader = None
  1114→    if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and SheetImageLoader:
  1115→        try:
  1116→            image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws)
  1117→        except Exception:
  1118→            pass
  1119→
  1120→    rows = parse_rows_dynamic(ws)  # Phase 4H: Dynamic column mapping
  1121→    tree = build_tree(rows)
  1122→
  1123→    if not tree:
  1124→        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))
  1125→
  1126→    project = doc.project
  1127→
  1128→    # Ensure items exist
  1129→    item_counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
  1130→    ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, item_counters)
  1131→
  1132→    # Deactivate old BOMs for confirmed items
  1133→    for item_code in confirmed_items:
  1134→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
  1135→            "Project Component Master",
  1136→            {
  1137→                "project": project,
  1138→                "item_code": item_code,
  1139→                "machine_code": machine_code
  1140→            },
  1141→            ["name", "active_bom"],
  1142→            as_dict=True
  1143→        )
  1144→        if cm and cm.active_bom:
  1145→            # Deactivate the old BOM (but keep active_bom reference for version change tracking)
  1146→            old_bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", cm.active_bom)
  1147→            old_bom.is_active = 0
  1148→            old_bom.is_default = 0
  1149→            old_bom.flags.ignore_validate = True
  1150→            old_bom.save(ignore_permissions=True)
  1151→            # NOTE: Don't clear active_bom here!
  1152→            # When new BOM is submitted, on_bom_submit will:
  1153→            # 1. Detect old_bom via component_master.active_bom
  1154→            # 2. Call _handle_bom_version_change() to log version history & remove old BOM usage
  1155→            # 3. Update active_bom to the new BOM via update_component_master_bom_fields()
  1156→
  1157→    # Build analysis with confirmed items treated as "new"
  1158→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
  1159→    confirmed_set = set(confirmed_items)
  1160→
  1161→    can_create = []
  1162→    for node in all_assemblies:
  1163→        item_code = node["item_code"]
  1164→
  1165→        # Check if already has active BOM (unchanged)
  1166→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
  1167→            "Project Component Master",
  1168→            {
  1169→                "project": project,
  1170→                "item_code": item_code,
  1171→                "machine_code": machine_code
  1172→            },
  1173→            ["active_bom", "bom_structure_hash"],
  1174→            as_dict=True
  1175→        )
  1176→
  1177→        if cm and cm.active_bom:
  1178→            # Confirmed items always get recreated (user explicitly confirmed)
  1179→            if item_code in confirmed_set:
  1180→                can_create.append(node)
  1181→                continue
  1182→
  1183→            # Already has active BOM - compare hashes using BOM's hash (source of truth)
  1184→            children = node.get("children", [])
  1185→            new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
  1186→
  1187→            # Get existing hash from BOM itself
  1188→            existing_hash = frappe.db.get_value(
  1189→                "BOM", cm.active_bom, "custom_bom_structure_hash"
  1190→            )
  1191→
  1192→            # Backfill if NULL
  1193→            if not existing_hash:
  1194→                bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", cm.active_bom)
  1195→                existing_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
  1196→                if existing_hash:
  1197→                    frappe.db.set_value(
  1198→                        "BOM", cm.active_bom,
  1199→                        "custom_bom_structure_hash", existing_hash,
  1200→                        update_modified=False
  1201→                    )
  1202→
  1203→            if new_hash == existing_hash:
  1204→                continue  # Unchanged, skip
  1205→
  1206→        # Either new, confirmed, or needs BOM
  1207→        can_create.append(node)
  1208→
  1209→    # Create BOMs
  1210→    analysis = {
  1211→        "can_create": can_create,
  1212→        "changed_components": [],
  1213→        "loose_blocked": [],
  1214→        "blocked_by_dependencies": {},
  1215→        "summary": {"can_create": len(can_create)},
  1216→    }
  1217→
  1218→    result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader)
  1219→
  1220→    frappe.db.commit()
  1221→    return result
  1222→
  1223→
  1224→# ==================== Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and Link ====================
  1225→
  1226→def create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader):
  1227→    """
  1228→    Create BOMs for components that passed analysis, then link active_bom
  1229→    back to their Component Masters.
  1230→
  1231→    Args:
  1232→        tree: List of root nodes
  1233→        project: Project name
  1234→        analysis: Analysis results dict
  1235→        ws: Excel worksheet
  1236→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
  1237→
  1238→    Returns:
  1239→        dict: {"status": "success", "created": X, "skipped": X, "failed": X, "errors": []}
  1240→    """
  1241→    created = 0
  1242→    skipped = 0
  1243→    failed = 0
  1244→    errors = []
  1245→
  1246→    # Only create BOMs for items in the can_create list
  1247→    can_create_codes = {n["item_code"] for n in analysis["can_create"]}
  1248→
  1249→    for node in tree:
  1250→        try:
  1251→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
  1252→            created += c
  1253→            skipped += s
  1254→            failed += f
  1255→            errors.extend(e)
  1256→        except Exception as ex:
  1257→            failed += 1
  1258→            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']}: {str(ex)}")
  1259→            frappe.log_error(
  1260→                title=f"BOM creation failed for {node['item_code']}",
  1261→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
  1262→            )
  1263→
  1264→    # Link active_bom back to Component Masters
  1265→    _link_boms_to_component_masters(project)
  1266→
  1267→    # Populate hierarchy codes (parent_component, m_code, g_code) for CMs with missing data
  1268→    # This runs after BOMs are linked so we have the full structure
  1269→    _populate_hierarchy_codes(project, only_missing=True)
  1270→
  1271→    # Recalculate all Component Master quantities (synchronous, after commit)
  1272→    # This ensures total_qty_limit and other calculated fields are populated
  1273→    from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import recalculate_component_masters_for_project
  1274→    recalc_result = recalculate_component_masters_for_project(project)
  1275→
  1276→    # Add recalculation info to errors if any failed
  1277→    if recalc_result.get("errors"):
  1278→        errors.extend(recalc_result["errors"])
  1279→
  1280→    return {
  1281→        "status": "success",
  1282→        "created": created,
  1283→        "skipped": skipped,
  1284→        "failed": failed,
  1285→        "errors": errors,
  1286→    }
  1287→
  1288→
  1289→def _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader):
  1290→    """
  1291→    Create BOM for a single node using existing create_bom_recursive.
  1292→    Only processes nodes whose item_code is in can_create_codes.
  1293→
  1294→    Args:
  1295→        node: Tree node dict
  1296→        project: Project name
  1297→        can_create_codes: Set of item codes allowed to create BOMs
  1298→        ws: Excel worksheet
  1299→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
  1300→
  1301→    Returns:
  1302→        tuple: (created, skipped, failed, errors)
  1303→    """
  1304→    created = 0
  1305→    skipped = 0
  1306→    failed = 0
  1307→    errors = []
  1308→
  1309→    item_code = node["item_code"]
  1310→
  1311→    if not node.get("children"):
  1312→        # Leaf node — no BOM needed
  1313→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
  1314→
  1315→    if item_code not in can_create_codes:
  1316→        skipped += 1
  1317→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
  1318→
  1319→    try:
  1320→        created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, project, ws, image_loader)
  1321→        if created_flag:
  1322→            created += 1
  1323→        else:
  1324→            skipped += 1
  1325→    except Exception as ex:
  1326→        failed += 1
  1327→        errors.append(f"{item_code}: {str(ex)}")
  1328→
  1329→    # Recurse into children
  1330→    for child in node.get("children", []):
  1331→        if child.get("children"):
  1332→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(child, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
  1333→            created += c
  1334→            skipped += s
  1335→            failed += f
  1336→            errors.extend(e)
  1337→
  1338→    return created, skipped, failed, errors
  1339→
  1340→
  1341→def _link_boms_to_component_masters(project):
  1342→    """
  1343→    After BOMs are created, link active_bom back to Component Masters.
  1344→    Also populate BOM Usage for linked BOMs (since on_bom_submit doesn't
  1345→    trigger for existing BOMs from other projects).
  1346→
  1347→    Fix (2026-02-04): Process ALL CMs with has_bom=1, not just those without
  1348→    active_bom. This handles the case where BOM is shared across projects -
  1349→    the BOM may have a different project set, so on_bom_submit wouldn't
  1350→    update this project's CM.
  1351→
  1352→    Design: BOMs are shared across projects (no duplicates). When a version
  1353→    change is needed, a new BOM is created only for the affected project.
  1354→    """
  1355→    # Find ALL Component Masters that have BOMs (not just those without active_bom)
  1356→    # This ensures we update active_bom even if it was pointing to an old/wrong BOM
  1357→    cms = frappe.get_all(
  1358→        "Project Component Master",
  1359→        filters={
  1360→            "project": project,
  1361→            "has_bom": 1,
  1362→            # Removed "active_bom not set" filter - process ALL CMs with has_bom=1
  1363→        },
  1364→        fields=["name", "item_code", "active_bom"],  # Include current active_bom
  1365→    )
  1366→
  1367→    # Track BOMs that were linked (not created) - need to populate BOM Usage for these
  1368→    linked_boms = []
  1369→
  1370→    for cm_data in cms:
  1371→        # Find the active default BOM for this item (check with project first)
  1372→        bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1373→            "BOM",
  1374→            {
  1375→                "item": cm_data.item_code,
  1376→                "project": project,
  1377→                "is_active": 1,
  1378→                "is_default": 1,
  1379→                "docstatus": 1,
  1380→            },
  1381→            "name",
  1382→        )
  1383→
  1384→        if not bom_name:
  1385→            # Also check without project filter (BOM may belong to another project
  1386→            # but can be shared - this is the "copy-on-write" approach)
  1387→            bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1388→                "BOM",
  1389→                {
  1390→                    "item": cm_data.item_code,
  1391→                    "is_active": 1,
  1392→                    "is_default": 1,
  1393→                    "docstatus": 1,
  1394→                },
  1395→                "name",
  1396→            )
  1397→
  1398→        if bom_name:
  1399→            # Only update if active_bom is different (avoid unnecessary saves)
  1400→            if cm_data.active_bom == bom_name:
  1401→                continue  # Already pointing to correct BOM, skip
  1402→
  1403→            cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_data.name)
  1404→            cm.active_bom = bom_name
  1405→
  1406→            # Recalculate hash from actual BOM doc
  1407→            try:
  1408→                bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name)
  1409→                cm.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
  1410→            except Exception:
  1411→                pass
  1412→
  1413→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
  1414→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
  1415→            cm.save(ignore_permissions=True)
  1416→
  1417→            # Track this BOM for BOM Usage population
  1418→            linked_boms.append({"name": bom_name, "item": cm_data.item_code})
  1419→
  1420→    # Populate BOM Usage for linked BOMs (since on_bom_submit didn't trigger)
  1421→    # This handles the case where BOMs already existed but Component Masters were just created
  1422→    if linked_boms:
  1423→        populate_bom_usage_tables(project, linked_boms)
  1424→
  1425→
  1426→def _populate_hierarchy_codes(project, only_missing=True):
  1427→    """
  1428→    Populate parent_component, m_code, g_code for Component Masters in a project.
  1429→
  1430→    This function is called after BOMs are linked to ensure hierarchy data is complete.
  1431→    It uses the BOM structure to derive:
  1432→    - parent_component: from active_bom's parent item
  1433→    - m_code: item starting with "M" in the hierarchy
  1434→    - g_code: item starting with "G" in the hierarchy
  1435→
  1436→    Args:
  1437→        project: Project name
  1438→        only_missing: If True, only update CMs where fields are NULL (default)
  1439→                      If False, force refresh ALL CMs
  1440→
  1441→    Returns:
  1442→        dict: {"updated": count, "skipped": count}
  1443→    """
  1444→    # Build filters based on only_missing flag
  1445→    if only_missing:
  1446→        # Get CMs where ANY of the hierarchy fields are missing
  1447→        cms = frappe.db.sql("""
  1448→            SELECT name, item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code
  1449→            FROM `tabProject Component Master`
  1450→            WHERE project = %(project)s
  1451→            AND (
  1452→                parent_component IS NULL OR parent_component = ''
  1453→                OR m_code IS NULL OR m_code = ''
  1454→                OR g_code IS NULL OR g_code = ''
  1455→            )
  1456→        """, {"project": project}, as_dict=True)
  1457→    else:
  1458→        cms = frappe.get_all(
  1459→            "Project Component Master",
  1460→            filters={"project": project},
  1461→            fields=["name", "item_code", "parent_component", "m_code", "g_code"]
  1462→        )
  1463→
  1464→    if not cms:
  1465→        return {"updated": 0, "skipped": 0}
  1466→
  1467→    updated = 0
  1468→    skipped = 0
  1469→
  1470→    # Build a lookup of item_code → CM name for parent lookups
  1471→    all_cms = frappe.get_all(
  1472→        "Project Component Master",
  1473→        filters={"project": project},
  1474→        fields=["name", "item_code"]
  1475→    )
  1476→    item_to_cm = {cm.item_code: cm.name for cm in all_cms}
  1477→
  1478→    for cm_data in cms:
  1479→        item_code = cm_data.item_code
  1480→        has_changes = False
  1481→
  1482→        # Derive m_code and g_code from item_code itself
  1483→        derived_m_code = None
  1484→        derived_g_code = None
  1485→
  1486→        if item_code.startswith("M"):
  1487→            # This IS an M-code
  1488→            derived_m_code = item_code
  1489→            derived_g_code = None
  1490→        elif item_code.startswith("G"):
  1491→            # This IS a G-code, need to find its M-code from parent
  1492→            derived_g_code = item_code
  1493→            # Find parent via BOM structure
  1494→            parent_item = _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project)
  1495→            if parent_item and parent_item.startswith("M"):
  1496→                derived_m_code = parent_item
  1497→            elif parent_item:
  1498→                # Parent is not M-code, try to get m_code from parent CM
  1499→                parent_cm_name = item_to_cm.get(parent_item)
  1500→                if parent_cm_name:
  1501→                    derived_m_code = frappe.db.get_value(
  1502→                        "Project Component Master", parent_cm_name, "m_code"
  1503→                    )
  1504→        else:
  1505→            # This is below G-level (D-code, raw material, etc.)
  1506→            # Find parent and derive both m_code and g_code
  1507→            parent_item = _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project)
  1508→            if parent_item:
  1509→                parent_cm_name = item_to_cm.get(parent_item)
  1510→                if parent_cm_name:
  1511→                    parent_cm_data = frappe.db.get_value(
  1512→                        "Project Component Master",
  1513→                        parent_cm_name,
  1514→                        ["m_code", "g_code", "item_code"],
  1515→                        as_dict=True
  1516→                    )
  1517→                    if parent_cm_data:
  1518→                        # Inherit m_code from parent
  1519→                        derived_m_code = parent_cm_data.m_code
  1520→
  1521→                        # If parent is G-code, use it as g_code
  1522→                        if parent_cm_data.item_code.startswith("G"):
  1523→                            derived_g_code = parent_cm_data.item_code
  1524→                        else:
  1525→                            # Otherwise inherit parent's g_code
  1526→                            derived_g_code = parent_cm_data.g_code
  1527→
  1528→        # Determine parent_component
  1529→        derived_parent_component = None
  1530→        parent_item = _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project)
  1531→        if parent_item:
  1532→            derived_parent_component = item_to_cm.get(parent_item)
  1533→
  1534→        # Check what needs updating
  1535→        needs_parent = (not cm_data.parent_component) and derived_parent_component
  1536→        needs_m_code = (not cm_data.m_code) and derived_m_code
  1537→        needs_g_code = (not cm_data.g_code) and derived_g_code
  1538→
  1539→        if needs_parent or needs_m_code or needs_g_code or not only_missing:
  1540→            # Update using db_set for efficiency (avoid full doc load/save)
  1541→            updates = {}
  1542→            if needs_parent or (not only_missing and derived_parent_component):
  1543→                updates["parent_component"] = derived_parent_component
  1544→            if needs_m_code or (not only_missing and derived_m_code):
  1545→                updates["m_code"] = derived_m_code
  1546→            if needs_g_code or (not only_missing and derived_g_code):
  1547→                updates["g_code"] = derived_g_code
  1548→
  1549→            if updates:
  1550→                frappe.db.set_value(
  1551→                    "Project Component Master",
  1552→                    cm_data.name,
  1553→                    updates,
  1554→                    update_modified=False
  1555→                )
  1556→                has_changes = True
  1557→
  1558→        if has_changes:
  1559→            updated += 1
  1560→        else:
  1561→            skipped += 1
  1562→
  1563→    if updated > 0:
  1564→        frappe.db.commit()
  1565→
  1566→    return {"updated": updated, "skipped": skipped}
  1567→
  1568→
  1569→def _find_parent_item_via_bom(item_code, project):
  1570→    """
  1571→    Find the parent item of an item by looking at BOM structure.
  1572→
  1573→    Checks which BOMs contain this item as a child and returns the parent item.
  1574→    Prioritizes BOMs with the same project, then any active BOM.
  1575→
  1576→    Args:
  1577→        item_code: Item code to find parent for
  1578→        project: Project name
  1579→
  1580→    Returns:
  1581→        str: Parent item code, or None if not found
  1582→    """
  1583→    # First try: Find BOM with matching project that contains this item
  1584→    parent_item = frappe.db.sql("""
  1585→        SELECT DISTINCT bom.item
  1586→        FROM `tabBOM` bom
  1587→        INNER JOIN `tabBOM Item` bi ON bi.parent = bom.name
  1588→        WHERE bi.item_code = %(item_code)s
  1589→        AND bom.project = %(project)s
  1590→        AND bom.docstatus = 1
  1591→        AND bom.is_active = 1
  1592→        LIMIT 1
  1593→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project})
  1594→
  1595→    if parent_item:
  1596→        return parent_item[0][0]
  1597→
  1598→    # Second try: Any active BOM that contains this item
  1599→    parent_item = frappe.db.sql("""
  1600→        SELECT DISTINCT bom.item
  1601→        FROM `tabBOM` bom
  1602→        INNER JOIN `tabBOM Item` bi ON bi.parent = bom.name
  1603→        WHERE bi.item_code = %(item_code)s
  1604→        AND bom.docstatus = 1
  1605→        AND bom.is_active = 1
  1606→        LIMIT 1
  1607→    """, {"item_code": item_code})
  1608→
  1609→    if parent_item:
  1610→        return parent_item[0][0]
  1611→
  1612→    return None
  1613→
  1614→
  1615→# ==================== Hash Calculation ====================
  1616→
  1617→def calculate_tree_structure_hash(children):
  1618→    """
  1619→    Create hash of BOM structure from tree node children (parsed from Excel).
  1620→    Uses same logic as bom_hooks.calculate_bom_structure_hash but works
  1621→    on tree node dicts instead of BOM doc items.
  1622→
  1623→    Args:
  1624→        children: List of child node dicts with item_code and qty
  1625→
  1626→    Returns:
  1627→        str: MD5 hash of sorted (item_code, qty) tuples
  1628→    """
  1629→    if not children:
  1630→        return None
  1631→
  1632→    structure = sorted(
  1633→        [(child["item_code"], float(child.get("qty", 1))) for child in children],
  1634→        key=lambda x: x[0],
  1635→    )
  1636→    structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True)
  1637→    return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest()
  1638→
  1639→
  1640→def calculate_bom_diff(old_bom_name, new_children):
  1641→    """
  1642→    Calculate diff between old BOM and new tree children.
  1643→    Shows what items were added, removed, or had qty changes.
  1644→
  1645→    Args:
  1646→        old_bom_name: Name of the old BOM
  1647→        new_children: List of child dicts from parsed Excel tree
  1648→
  1649→    Returns:
  1650→        dict: {
  1651→            "added": [{"item_code": str, "qty": float}, ...],
  1652→            "removed": [{"item_code": str, "qty": float}, ...],
  1653→            "qty_changed": [{"item_code": str, "old_qty": float, "new_qty": float}, ...]
  1654→        }
  1655→    """
  1656→    # Get old BOM items (consolidated)
  1657→    old_items = {}
  1658→    if old_bom_name:
  1659→        try:
  1660→            old_bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", old_bom_name)
  1661→            for item in old_bom_doc.items:
  1662→                if item.item_code in old_items:
  1663→                    old_items[item.item_code] += float(item.qty or 0)
  1664→                else:
  1665→                    old_items[item.item_code] = float(item.qty or 0)
  1666→        except frappe.DoesNotExistError:
  1667→            pass
  1668→
  1669→    # Get new items from tree children (consolidated)
  1670→    new_items = {}
  1671→    for child in (new_children or []):
  1672→        item_code = child.get("item_code")
  1673→        qty = float(child.get("qty", 0))
  1674→        if item_code in new_items:
  1675→            new_items[item_code] += qty
  1676→        else:
  1677→            new_items[item_code] = qty
  1678→
  1679→    old_set = set(old_items.keys())
  1680→    new_set = set(new_items.keys())
  1681→
  1682→    added = [{"item_code": ic, "qty": new_items[ic]} for ic in sorted(new_set - old_set)]
  1683→    removed = [{"item_code": ic, "qty": old_items[ic]} for ic in sorted(old_set - new_set)]
  1684→    qty_changed = []
  1685→
  1686→    for ic in sorted(old_set & new_set):
  1687→        if abs(old_items[ic] - new_items[ic]) > 0.0001:  # Float comparison tolerance
  1688→            qty_changed.append({
  1689→                "item_code": ic,
  1690→                "old_qty": old_items[ic],
  1691→                "new_qty": new_items[ic]
  1692→            })
  1693→
  1694→    return {
  1695→        "added": added,
  1696→        "removed": removed,
  1697→        "qty_changed": qty_changed
  1698→    }
  1699→
  1700→
  1701→# ==================== Summary Message ====================
  1702→
  1703→def _build_summary_message(summary, errors=None):
  1704→    """
  1705→    Build a human-readable summary message.
  1706→
  1707→    Args:
  1708→        summary: dict with items, boms, component_masters sections
  1709→        errors: list of error strings
  1710→
  1711→    Returns:
  1712→        str: Formatted summary message
  1713→    """
  1714→    parts = []
  1715→
  1716→    items = summary.get("items", {})
  1717→    if items.get("created"):
  1718→        parts.append(f"{items['created']} Items created")
  1719→    if items.get("existing"):
  1720→        parts.append(f"{items['existing']} Items already existed")
  1721→
  1722→    boms = summary.get("boms", {})
  1723→    if boms.get("created"):
  1724→        parts.append(f"{boms['created']} BOMs created")
  1725→    if boms.get("existing"):
  1726→        parts.append(f"{boms['existing']} BOMs already existed")
  1727→
  1728→    cms = summary.get("component_masters", {})
  1729→    if cms.get("created"):
  1730→        parts.append(f"{cms['created']} Component Masters created")
  1731→    if cms.get("existing"):
  1732→        parts.append(f"{cms['existing']} Component Masters already existed")
  1733→    if cms.get("updated"):
  1734→        parts.append(f"{cms['updated']} Component Masters updated")
  1735→
  1736→    # Add failure warnings
  1737→    failures = items.get("failed", 0) + boms.get("failed", 0) + cms.get("failed", 0)
  1738→    if failures:
  1739→        parts.append(f"⚠️ {failures} failures")
  1740→
  1741→    if errors and len(errors) > 0:
  1742→        parts.append(f"⚠️ {len(errors)} errors (check error log)")
  1743→
  1744→    return ". ".join(parts) + "." if parts else "No changes made."
  1745→
  1746→
  1747→# ==================== Serialization ====================
  1748→
  1749→def _serialize_analysis(analysis):
  1750→    """
  1751→    Serialize analysis dict for JSON response to client.
  1752→    Convert node dicts to safe serializable format.
  1753→    """
  1754→    def serialize_node(node):
  1755→        return {
  1756→            "item_code": node.get("item_code"),
  1757→            "description": node.get("description"),
  1758→            "level": node.get("level"),
  1759→            "qty": node.get("qty"),
  1760→        }
  1761→
  1762→    def serialize_details(details):
  1763→        """Serialize details dict, ensuring procurement_docs is serializable."""
  1764→        if not details:
  1765→            return {}
  1766→        return {
  1767→            "change_type": details.get("change_type"),
  1768→            "component_master": details.get("component_master"),
  1769→            "old_bom": details.get("old_bom"),
  1770→            "blocking_level": details.get("blocking_level"),
  1771→            "blocking_message": details.get("blocking_message"),
  1772→            "can_proceed": details.get("can_proceed"),
  1773→            "procurement_docs": details.get("procurement_docs", []),
  1774→        }
  1775→
  1776→    serialized = {
  1777→        "can_create": [serialize_node(n) for n in analysis.get("can_create", [])],
  1778→        "changed_components": [],
  1779→        "loose_blocked": [],
  1780→        "blocked_by_dependencies": analysis.get("blocked_by_dependencies", {}),
  1781→        "summary": analysis.get("summary", {}),
  1782→    }
  1783→
  1784→    for comp in analysis.get("changed_components", []):
  1785→        serialized["changed_components"].append({
  1786→            "node": serialize_node(comp.get("node", {})),
  1787→            "details": serialize_details(comp.get("details", {})),
  1788→        })
  1789→
  1790→    for comp in analysis.get("loose_blocked", []):
  1791→        serialized["loose_blocked"].append({
  1792→            "node": serialize_node(comp.get("node", {})),
  1793→            "details": comp.get("details", {}),
  1794→        })
  1795→
  1796→    return serialized
  1797→
  1798→
  1799→# ==================== Debug Functions ====================
  1800→
  1801→@frappe.whitelist()
  1802→def debug_bom_quantities(docname, target_item_code=None):
  1803→    """
  1804→    Debug function to trace BOM quantity calculations without creating BOMs.
  1805→
  1806→    Simulates the BOM creation process and reports how quantities are calculated
  1807→    for a specific item code, helping identify discrepancies between Excel and BOM.
  1808→
  1809→    Args:
  1810→        docname: BOM Upload document name
  1811→        target_item_code: Specific item code to trace (optional, if None traces all)
  1812→
  1813→    Returns:
  1814→        dict: {
  1815→            "target_item": str,
  1816→            "excel_total_qty": float,
  1817→            "excel_occurrences": int,
  1818→            "occurrences_by_parent": [
  1819→                {
  1820→                    "parent_item": str,
  1821→                    "parent_level": int,
  1822→                    "occurrences": [
  1823→                        {
  1824→                            "row": int,
  1825→                            "position": str,
  1826→                            "qty": float,
  1827→                            "level": int
  1828→                        }
  1829→                    ],
  1830→                    "total_qty": float,
  1831→                    "bom_items_count": int  # How many BOM items would be created
  1832→                }
  1833→            ],
  1834→            "tree_analysis": str  # Human-readable analysis
  1835→        }
  1836→    """
  1837→    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
  1838→
  1839→    if not doc.bom_file:
  1840→        frappe.throw(_("Please attach a BOM Excel file first."))
  1841→
  1842→    # Step 1: Parse Excel
  1843→    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
  1844→    content = file_doc.get_content()
  1845→
  1846→    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
  1847→    ws = wb.active
  1848→
  1849→    rows = parse_rows_dynamic(ws)
  1850→    tree = build_tree(rows)
  1851→
  1852→    if not tree:
  1853→        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))
  1854→
  1855→    # Step 2: If no target specified, find most common item for demo
  1856→    if not target_item_code:
  1857→        # Count item occurrences
  1858→        item_counts = {}
  1859→        def count_items(nodes):
  1860→            for node in nodes:
  1861→                item_counts[node["item_code"]] = item_counts.get(node["item_code"], 0) + 1
  1862→                if node["children"]:
  1863→                    count_items(node["children"])
  1864→        count_items(tree)
  1865→
  1866→        # Find item that appears most frequently
  1867→        if item_counts:
  1868→            target_item_code = max(item_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])[0]
  1869→        else:
  1870→            frappe.throw(_("No items found in Excel."))
  1871→
  1872→    # Step 3: Trace all occurrences of target item
  1873→    occurrences_by_parent = {}
  1874→    excel_total_qty = 0
  1875→    excel_occurrences_count = 0
  1876→
  1877→    def trace_item_in_tree(parent_node, parent_stack):
  1878→        """
  1879→        Recursively trace occurrences of target item.
  1880→
  1881→        Args:
  1882→            parent_node: Current node being examined
  1883→            parent_stack: List of parent nodes leading to this node
  1884→        """
  1885→        nonlocal excel_total_qty, excel_occurrences_count
  1886→
  1887→        # Check if any direct children match target item
  1888→        for child in parent_node["children"]:
  1889→            if child["item_code"] == target_item_code:
  1890→                # Found an occurrence
  1891→                excel_total_qty += child["qty"]
  1892→                excel_occurrences_count += 1
  1893→
  1894→                # Track by parent assembly
  1895→                parent_key = parent_node["item_code"]
  1896→                if parent_key not in occurrences_by_parent:
  1897→                    occurrences_by_parent[parent_key] = {
  1898→                        "parent_item": parent_node["item_code"],
  1899→                        "parent_description": parent_node.get("description", ""),
  1900→                        "parent_level": parent_node["level"],
  1901→                        "occurrences": [],
  1902→                        "total_qty": 0
  1903→                    }
  1904→
  1905→                occurrences_by_parent[parent_key]["occurrences"].append({
  1906→                    "row": child.get("row_num", "?"),
  1907→                    "position": child.get("position", "?"),
  1908→                    "qty": child["qty"],
  1909→                    "level": child["level"],
  1910→                    "parent_chain": " → ".join([p["item_code"] for p in parent_stack] + [parent_node["item_code"]])
  1911→                })
  1912→                occurrences_by_parent[parent_key]["total_qty"] += child["qty"]
  1913→
  1914→            # Recurse into child if it has children (is an assembly)
  1915→            if child["children"]:
  1916→                trace_item_in_tree(child, parent_stack + [parent_node])
  1917→
  1918→    # Start tracing from all root nodes
  1919→    for root in tree:
  1920→        if root["children"]:
  1921→            trace_item_in_tree(root, [])
  1922→
  1923→    # Step 4: Calculate how many BOM items would be created per parent
  1924→    for parent_key, data in occurrences_by_parent.items():
  1925→        # In current implementation, each occurrence creates a separate BOM item
  1926→        # (no consolidation happens in build_tree or create_bom_recursive)
  1927→        data["bom_items_count"] = len(data["occurrences"])
  1928→
  1929→    # Step 5: Generate human-readable analysis
  1930→    analysis_lines = []
  1931→    analysis_lines.append(f"=== BOM Quantity Debug Report ===")
  1932→    analysis_lines.append(f"Target Item: {target_item_code}")
  1933→    analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1934→    analysis_lines.append(f"Excel Analysis:")
  1935→    analysis_lines.append(f"  Total Occurrences: {excel_occurrences_count}")
  1936→    analysis_lines.append(f"  Total Quantity (sum): {excel_total_qty}")
  1937→    analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1938→    analysis_lines.append(f"Occurrences by Parent Assembly:")
  1939→    analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1940→
  1941→    for parent_key in sorted(occurrences_by_parent.keys()):
  1942→        data = occurrences_by_parent[parent_key]
  1943→        analysis_lines.append(f"Parent: {data['parent_item']} (Level {data['parent_level']})")
  1944→        analysis_lines.append(f"  Description: {data['parent_description']}")
  1945→        analysis_lines.append(f"  Occurrences: {len(data['occurrences'])}")
  1946→        analysis_lines.append(f"  Total Qty: {data['total_qty']}")
  1947→        analysis_lines.append(f"  BOM Items to be Created: {data['bom_items_count']}")
  1948→        analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1949→
  1950→        for i, occ in enumerate(data["occurrences"], 1):
  1951→            analysis_lines.append(f"    #{i}: Row {occ['row']}, Pos={occ['position']}, Qty={occ['qty']}, Level={occ['level']}")
  1952→            analysis_lines.append(f"        Hierarchy: {occ['parent_chain']}")
  1953→
  1954→        analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1955→
  1956→        # CRITICAL: Explain how BOM creation works
  1957→        if data['bom_items_count'] > 1:
  1958→            analysis_lines.append(f"  ⚠️  IMPORTANT: {data['parent_item']} will have {data['bom_items_count']} SEPARATE")
  1959→            analysis_lines.append(f"      BOM item entries for {target_item_code} (one per Excel row).")
  1960→            analysis_lines.append(f"      Frappe BOM may or may not consolidate these - need to check actual BOM!")
  1961→            analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1962→
  1963→    analysis_lines.append(f"=== How BOM Creation Works ===")
  1964→    analysis_lines.append(f"1. build_tree() appends each Excel row to parent's children[] (NO consolidation)")
  1965→    analysis_lines.append(f"2. create_bom_recursive() iterates children[] and adds each as BOM item")
  1966→    analysis_lines.append(f"3. If same item appears 3 times in Excel → 3 entries in children[] → 3 BOM items")
  1967→    analysis_lines.append(f"4. Frappe may consolidate duplicate item_code entries in BOM UI (TBD - check actual BOM)")
  1968→    analysis_lines.append(f"")
  1969→
  1970→    # Convert to list for JSON serialization
  1971→    occurrences_list = [data for data in occurrences_by_parent.values()]
  1972→
  1973→    return {
  1974→        "target_item": target_item_code,
  1975→        "excel_total_qty": excel_total_qty,
  1976→        "excel_occurrences": excel_occurrences_count,
  1977→        "occurrences_by_parent": occurrences_list,
  1978→        "tree_analysis": "\n".join(analysis_lines),
  1979→        "parents_count": len(occurrences_by_parent)
  1980→    }

1→""" 2→BOM Event Hooks for Project Component Master 3→ 4→Handles updating BOM Usage child table when BOMs are created, updated, or cancelled. 5→Uses "silent skip" pattern - only updates if Component Master exists for the component. 6→Shows info message when project BOMs are not tracked (helps catch accidental omissions). 7→""" 8→ 9→import frappe 10→from frappe import _ 11→import hashlib 12→import json 13→ 14→ 15→def on_bom_validate(doc, method=None): 16→ """ 17→ Called during BOM validation (before submit). 18→ Implements tiered blocking for BOM version changes. 19→ 20→ If a different BOM was already active for this item+project: 21→ - Checks child items for active MR/RFQ/PO 22→ - Blocks submission if procurement exists 23→ - Guides user to deactivate old BOM first (uncheck "Is Active") 24→ 25→ This prevents BOM version changes when procurement is in progress. 26→ """ 27→ if not doc.project: 28→ # No project linked - skip validation (standard product BOM) 29→ return 30→ 31→ # Check if this BOM is tracked in Component Master 32→ component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) 33→ if not component_master: 34→ # Not tracked - skip validation 35→ return 36→ 37→ # Check for BOM version change 38→ old_bom = component_master.active_bom 39→ if not old_bom or old_bom == doc.name: 40→ # No version change, or same BOM being resubmitted 41→ return 42→ 43→ # BOM version change detected - check if old BOM is still active 44→ try: 45→ old_bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", old_bom) 46→ if old_bom_doc.is_active: 47→ # Old BOM is still active - check for procurement blocking 48→ blocking_result = _check_bom_version_blocking(doc.project, old_bom) 49→ 50→ if not blocking_result["can_proceed"]: 51→ # Build error message with procurement details 52→ error_msg = _( 53→ "Cannot submit new BOM version while old BOM is active.

" 54→ "Another BOM ({0}) is currently active for item {1} in project {2}.

# ==================== Step 3: Create Component Masters ====================

def create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, project, machine_code):
    """
    Create Component Masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf/RM items).
    For existing CMs: apply make/buy merge logic (Excel wins if present, else keep existing).
    For new CMs: set defaults based on item type.

    Business Rules:
    - All items default to "Buy" (conservative approach)
    - T, Y, E prefixed items: released_for_procurement = "No"
    - Other items: released_for_procurement = "Yes"
    - machine_code is set on all Component Masters (enables per-machine tracking)

    Args:
        tree: List of root nodes
        project: Project name
        machine_code: Machine code from BOM Upload (e.g., P0000000003033)

    Returns:
        dict: {"created": X, "existing": X, "updated": X, "failed": X}
    """
    counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
    all_nodes = _get_all_nodes(tree)

    for node in all_nodes:
        item_code = node["item_code"]
        is_assembly = bool(node.get("children"))

        # Check if Component Master already exists (project + item_code + machine_code unique)
        existing = frappe.db.exists(
            "Project Component Master",
            {
                "project": project,
                "item_code": item_code,
                "machine_code": machine_code
            },
        )

        if existing:
            # Merge logic: update make_or_buy only if Excel has a value
            updated = _merge_make_or_buy(existing, node)
            if updated:
                counters["updated"] += 1
            else:
                counters["existing"] += 1
            continue

        try:
            # Check if item starts with T, Y, or E
            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
            if item_code_upper.startswith(("T", "Y", "E")):
                released_for_procurement = "No"
            else:
                released_for_procurement = "Yes"

            # Only set project_qty for root assemblies (level 1)
            # Child items (level 2+) get project_qty=0, their requirement comes from bom_qty_required
            level = node.get("level", 0)
            project_qty = node.get("qty", 0) if level == 1 else 0

            cm_data = {
                "doctype": "Project Component Master",
                "project": project,
                "item_code": item_code,
                "machine_code": machine_code,  # Set machine code on all Component Masters
                "bom_level": level,
                "is_loose_item": 0,
                "design_status": "Design Released",
                "project_qty": project_qty,  # From Excel column E (root assembly only)
                "created_from": "BOM Upload",
                "released_for_procurement": released_for_procurement,
            }

            # Determine Make/Buy based on item code prefix
            # Items starting with M or G: "Make" (assemblies/sub-assemblies)
            # All other items: "Buy" (raw materials/purchased components)
            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
            if item_code_upper.startswith(("M", "G")):
                default_make_or_buy = "Make"
            else:
                default_make_or_buy = "Buy"

            if is_assembly:
                cm_data["has_bom"] = 1
                cm_data["active_bom"] = None  # Set later after BOM creation
                cm_data["bom_structure_hash"] = calculate_tree_structure_hash(node["children"])
                # Excel value takes precedence, otherwise use prefix-based default
                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or default_make_or_buy
            else:
                # Leaf item (raw material) - use prefix-based default
                cm_data["has_bom"] = 0
                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or default_make_or_buy

            cm = frappe.get_doc(cm_data)
            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
            cm.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
            counters["created"] += 1
        except Exception:
            counters["failed"] += 1
            frappe.log_error(
                title=f"Failed to create Component Master for {item_code}",
                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
            )

    return counters


def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):

OK so the code confirms:
- Root (level 1): `project_qty = node.get("qty", 0)` — from Excel column E
- Non-root (level 2+): `project_qty = 0`

The user is saying "for non root it gets set from BOM-usage table right?" — they seem to believe non-root project_qty comes from bom_usage. But the code says `project_qty = 0` for non-root. What the user might be referring to is `bom_qty_required` (which IS calculated from bom_usage), not `project_qty`.

Or wait — maybe the user is asking about what WAS the situation before (when it was working). Maybe previously, `project_qty` for non-root was also being set from something? Or maybe they're conflating `project_qty` with `bom_qty_required` or `total_qty_limit`.
 
I need to verify whether `project_qty` gets populated anywhere else in the code for non-root items, and trace back how the tree parsing initially sets `node.qty` and `node.level`. The user's specific question is whether `project_qty` comes from column E for root items and from the BOM-usage table for non-root items. Looking at the actual code, non-root `project_qty` is hardcoded to 0 at line 469 — it doesn't pull from bom_usage at all. The value that actually comes from bom_usage is `bom_qty_required`, which gets calculated separately, so the user might be mixing up which field gets populated from where. So the answer is:
- Root: `project_qty` = column E value ✓
- Non-root: `project_qty` = 0 (hard-coded). It does NOT come from bom_usage.
- What comes from bom_usage is `bom_qty_required` = SUM(parent.total_qty_limit × qty_per_unit)
- The actual procurement limit is `total_qty_limit` = MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required)

For non-root: `total_qty_limit` = MAX(0, bom_qty_required) = bom_qty_required

So the field the user might be thinking of is `bom_qty_required` or `total_qty_limit`, not `project_qty` itself.

def parse_rows_dynamic(ws):
    """
    Parse Excel rows using dynamic column mapping (handles format changes).
    Replacement for hardcoded parse_rows() from bom_upload.py (Phase 4H).

    Uses map_excel_columns() to find column positions by header name instead of
    hardcoded positions. This handles PE2 export format changes automatically.

    Args:
        ws: Excel worksheet

    Returns:
        list: List of row dicts with parsed data (same format as parse_rows)
    """
    # Get dynamic column mapping (validates required columns exist)
    col_map = map_excel_columns(ws)

    rows = []

    # Start from row 3 (row 1 = title, row 2 = headers, row 3+ = data)
    for r in range(3, ws.max_row + 1):
        # Check if item_code exists (required field)
        code_raw = ws[f"{col_map['item_code']}{r}"].value
        if not code_raw:
            continue  # Skip empty rows

        # Read position (column A or B fallback, same as original)
        position_col = col_map.get("position", "A")
        position = ws[f"{position_col}{r}"].value or ws[f"B{r}"].value

        rows.append({
            "row_num": r,  # Store row number for image extraction
            "position": position,
            "item_code": clean_code(code_raw),
            "description": ws[f"{col_map['description']}{r}"].value,
            "extended_description": ws[f"{col_map['extended_description']}{r}"].value,
            "qty": to_float(ws[f"{col_map['qty']}{r}"].value, 1),
            "revision": ws[f"{col_map['revision']}{r}"].value,
            "material": ws[f"{col_map['material']}{r}"].value,
            "part_number": ws[f"{col_map['part_number']}{r}"].value,
            "weight": to_float(ws[f"{col_map['weight']}{r}"].value, 0),
            "manufacturer": ws[f"{col_map['manufacturer']}{r}"].value,
            "treatment": ws[f"{col_map['treatment']}{r}"].value,
            "uom": normalize_uom(ws[f"{col_map['uom']}{r}"].value),
            "level": int(ws[f"{col_map['level']}{r}"].value or 0),  # CRITICAL!
            "children": []
        })

    return rows


# ==================== Main Entry Point ====================

@frappe.whitelist()
def create_boms_with_validation(docname):
    """
    Enhanced BOM creation with validation and Component Master integration.

    Args:
        docname: BOM Upload document name

    Returns:
        dict: Analysis results or creation summary
    """
    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)

    if not doc.bom_file:
        frappe.throw(_("Please attach a BOM Excel file first."))

    if not doc.project:
        frappe.throw(_("Please select a Project first."))

    # Validate machine_code (required for new uploads)
    machine_code = doc.machine_code
    if not machine_code:
        frappe.throw(_("Machine Code is required for BOM Upload. Please enter the machine code (e.g., P0000000003033)."))

    # Step 1: Parse Excel (reuse existing logic)
    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
    content = file_doc.get_content()

    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
    ws = wb.active

    # Initialize image loader only if available
    image_loader = None
    if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and SheetImageLoader:
        try:
            image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws)
        except Exception:

# ================= Parse Excel =================

def parse_rows(ws):

    rows = []

    for r in range(3, ws.max_row + 1):

        code_raw = ws[f"C{r}"].value
        if not code_raw:
            continue

        position = ws[f"A{r}"].value or ws[f"B{r}"].value

        rows.append({
            "row_num": r,  # Store row number for image extraction
            "position": position,
            "item_code": clean_code(code_raw),
            "description": ws[f"D{r}"].value,
            "extended_description": ws[f"U{r}"].value,  # DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA for item description
            "qty": to_float(ws[f"E{r}"].value, 1),
            "revision": ws[f"G{r}"].value,
            "material": ws[f"AD{r}"].value,
            "part_number": ws[f"AE{r}"].value,
            "weight": to_float(ws[f"AF{r}"].value, 0),
            "manufacturer": ws[f"AG{r}"].value,
            "treatment": ws[f"AL{r}"].value,
            "uom": normalize_uom(ws[f"AN{r}"].value),
            "level": int(ws[f"AR{r}"].value or 0),
            "children": []
        })

    return rows


# ================= Hierarchy =================

def build_tree(rows):

    stack = []
    roots = []

    for row in rows:

        while stack and stack[-1]["level"] >= row["level"]:
            stack.pop()
        if stack:
            stack[-1]["children"].append(row)
        else:
            roots.append(row)

        stack.append(row)

    return roots


# ================= Items =================
